manifest_note: Per-field derivation contract for the fields the interview produces. Each entry says HOW a field is derived (derivation_class), whether it is an operator decision (decision_field + decision_kind), the value's shape + constraints, which interview question-IDs feed it (source_question_ids), and which foundation doc renders it (preview_doc). Coupling rule: a classification or policy field is always a decision (decision_field true), and decision_field is true exactly when decision_kind is not 'none'. The interview maps logical groups (see derivation-groups) to question-IDs; this manifest is the finer field-level mapping the derivation step reads so no field knowledge is hardcoded in the barrier. preview_doc is the doc a field's value renders into (which may belong to a group whose barrier fires later than the field's own group); '' means the field is not a foundation-doc template placeholder (it is consumed at the plan level, by a later group's derivation, or is a global).

fields.note: the operator's stated constraints are authored into the vision narrative here (system voice, grounded in their answers); elevating any to a top-level always-ask policy is the separate TIER_1_ADDITIONS policy derivation at the vision barrier

constraint-elevation: scan the operator's confirmed vision constraints and propose always-stop-and-ask rules to add to the Tier-1 baseline (forced confirmation; policy class). Fires only when the constraints contain elevatable rules; consumed by the HITL policy derivation. Not a mandatory group target field.
rendered from the derived agent intents; the agent layer itself is assembled deterministically from the agent-intent derivation

the confirmed advisor list (ADV-1) extracted into advisor-knowledge-base header entries; fills the {{ADVISOR_ENTRIES}} placeholder the generator emits into the advisor knowledge base. Empty when the operator confirms no advisors (matches the zero-advisors scaffold). Not a foundation-doc placeholder (the advisor KB is a quality/ scaffold template), so preview_doc is empty.

THE single canonical capture of the system's external dependencies (DEP-1, step 09): a JSON array, one object per dependency {id (immutable slug), name, type (allow 'Unknown'), roles (>=1 of boundary_input/boundary_output/health_monitored/needs_credential)} plus a credential_facet {env_var, cred_type, provider, provisional_expiry} for each needs_credential dependency. decision_field=true (integration_boundary) — adding, dropping, renaming, or re-roling a dependency changes what the system validates, monitors, connects to, and sends out through, so a later edit must surface for operator disposition. This is the decision surface; the three tabular registries are non-authoritative projection views of it. boundary_input/health_monitored/needs_credential each drive a projection; boundary_output (the system sends data/notifications OUT through it — symmetric partner to boundary_input) drives no registry at v0 but keeps the dependency in the record and in INTEGRATIONS. A zero-role dependency is invalid (it declares no relationship to the system) — validity is 'declares a relationship', not 'projects into a registry', so a boundary_output-only dependency is valid; zero dependencies is a valid empty record. Renders into no foundation doc (the projections do); reviewed inline at the step-09 barrier.

content-only narrative companion to the identity record: a JSON array {id, purpose, what_stops, boundary_input_facet {input_risk} for boundary_input deps, health_facet for health_monitored deps}. purpose/what_stops captured at step 09 (DEP-1); the boundary_input facet enriched at step 11 (GATE-1) and the health facet at step 13 (QA-3), scoped to the dependencies already in the identity record (no orphaned facets). content-only: a narrative edit is wording, not an integration-boundary decision (that lives on the identity record). Renders into no foundation doc; closes at step_13.

the human-readable integration/data-source prose list, authored from the canonical dependency record (runtime _derivation_inputs [EXTERNAL_DEPENDENCY_IDENTITY]). synthesis, not projection: it authors a prose list, not a table, so a pure-code projection cannot produce it. Re-pointed to the canonical record so it never drifts from the registries.

non-authoritative projection: the needs_credential subset of the canonical dependency record (runtime _derivation_inputs [EXTERNAL_DEPENDENCY_IDENTITY]), reshaped into pre-populated Pending rows for security/credentials_registry.md. Metadata only, never values; rows ship Status: Pending and the first-boot credential-setup skill moves them to Active. Computed by deterministic code, not authored. Scaffold-rendered (preview_doc '').

operator's no-expiry re-check cadence (CRED-4). Scaffold-rendered into project_instructions.md + docs/future_items.md; overrides the scaffold default. RW-37: previously unwired (scaffold default 'quarterly' always won).

operator's expiry-warning lead time in days (CRED-3). Scaffold-rendered into project_instructions.md; the credential-check mechanism warns this many days before expiry (test_cases AC-C-6). RW-37: previously had no emitted placeholder at all.
compliance-gap prose synthesized from the external dependencies (DEP-1, the canonical record) + concurrency posture (CONC-2).

THE single structured source of the system's planned capability increments (the MVP<->roadmap release-boundary decision): a JSON array, one object per increment {capability, agents, phase (int, for committed work), release_bucket (mvp | post_mvp_roadmap | candidate_conditional), depends_on?, rationale?, condition? (required for candidate_conditional)}. synthesis from the approach brief + roster + orchestration model + vision scope/success + the operator's deferred / 'maybe-later' early mentions; runtime _derivation_inputs [APPROACH_SOLUTION_BRIEF, AGENT_ROSTER_ROWS, ORCHESTRATION_MODEL, VISION_SCOPE_BOUNDARY, VISION_SUCCESS_CRITERIA]. decision_field=true (release_boundary) — which capabilities are in the MVP vs deferred to the roadmap vs only-maybe-later is an operator decision (forced confirm). This is the decision surface; BUILD_PHASES_ROWS + MVP_ROADMAP_BOUNDARY are deterministic projection views of it and the MVP_* prose derives from it, so the emitted execution_plan cannot contradict itself (was: MVP_* and BUILD_PHASES_ROWS synthesized independently -> derivation-luck boundary). candidate_conditional carries no phase (not committed). Out-of-scope is NOT here (it lives in vision Scope Boundary).

deterministic projection: the committed increments (mvp + post_mvp_roadmap) of the canonical CAPABILITY_INCREMENTS record, grouped by phase into the build-sequence table body. runtime _derivation_inputs [CAPABILITY_INCREMENTS]. Computed in pure code (capability_projection.py), never authored — a view of the confirmed source, so it cannot drift from the MVP/roadmap boundary. candidate_conditional increments are excluded (not committed). Was synthesis (independently derived from P1-4/ARCH-1); flipped to projection so it can no longer drift from the boundary.

deterministic projection: the canonical CAPABILITY_INCREMENTS record split into the operator-facing 'Delivered in the MVP' / 'On the roadmap' / 'Possible later' buckets for the execution_plan MVP-and-Roadmap-Boundary section. runtime _derivation_inputs [CAPABILITY_INCREMENTS]. Computed in pure code (capability_projection.py). No-tail state (no deferred work) renders 'nothing is deferred'. The static 'Not included -> Vision Scope Boundary' cross-reference is owned by the template, not this projection.

fields.authority_note: Derived from the operator authority profile (operator-preference class): the autonomy level is a ceiling/min of the operator's desired autonomy (UP-3 decision preference + UP-5 involvement) capped by domain risk (DR), reversibility tolerance (REV), and trust posture (probationary at first build, lifting over time). Routine action classes stay autonomous even at level 1; the derived level + the resulting ask-first rules are confirmed against the execution-plan preview.

fields.note: the human-in-the-loop policy (what acts autonomously vs asks first). Policy class: derived from prior FIELDS via runtime _derivation_inputs — AUTONOMY_LEVEL (mandatory; the authority profile's per-class posture) PLUS TIER_1_ADDITIONS WHEN the vision barrier produced it (optional field; cite only when present, else DR-8 fails) — PLUS the operator's explicit always-ask summary (ARCH-4, the one answer-level source). MUST state explicit negative permissions. (Was a kitchen-sink FIN-1/FIN-3/FIN-4/ERR/CONC source set pre-authority-profile; reconciled to the real field-level inputs at RW-46.)

included monthly automation-credit dollar pool, looked up from PLAN_TYPE (Pro $20 / Max5x $100 / Max20x $200 / Team-Std $20 / Team-Premium $100; verified 2026-06-07, effective 2026-06-15, re-verify before relying — volatile). Renders into project_instructions.md + cost_efficiency_log.md (scaffold), not a foundation-doc placeholder.

operator-preference; how much of the per-user automation credit this project may use when several systems share it. Fairness-only (the included pool is a platform hard-boundary, no real-money risk); rebalanced when the wizard builds system #2.

deterministic financial projection: round(AUTOMATION_CREDIT_POOL × share fraction; sole 0.9 / one-of-several 0.4), the enforceable monthly automation budget rendered into project_instructions.md + cost_efficiency_log.md. runtime _derivation_inputs [AUTOMATION_CREDIT_POOL, PROJECT_SHARE_POSTURE]. Computed in pure code (financial_projection.py), never authored by the model — money safety-envelope; keys the cost-log alert thresholds. No separate confirm (auto/deterministic); the operator's plain choices were confirmed at step 02.

operator-preference; what the system does when the automation budget is used up (replaces the retired OVERAGE_PLAN_TYPE). paid-overflow requires platform usage-credits + a spending cap (PAYG_CAP); Team non-admins route to wait.

operator-preference, CONDITIONAL (present only when EXHAUSTION_BEHAVIOR == paid-overflow; absent otherwise — like TIER_1_ADDITIONS, not a mandatory group target). Dollar cap on paid overflow. The AUTHORITATIVE cap is the operator's Anthropic platform spending limit; this is the generated system's early-stop guard (conservative estimate + safety margin).

deterministic financial projection: max(1, round(0.10 × PROJECT_AUTOMATION_BUDGET)); one estimated-expensive operation above this pauses for operator approval. runtime _derivation_inputs [PROJECT_AUTOMATION_BUDGET]. Computed in pure code (financial_projection.py), never authored by the model. No separate confirm (auto/deterministic).

non-authoritative projection: the boundary_input subset of the canonical dependency record (runtime _derivation_inputs [EXTERNAL_DEPENDENCY_IDENTITY, EXTERNAL_DEPENDENCY_ANNOTATION]), reshaped into the 6-col validation-gate inventory and scaffold-rendered into quality/validation_gate_config.md + project_instructions.md. The integration-boundary decision lives on the canonical identity record (dropping/re-roling an input surfaces there); this is a generated view of it. Computed by deterministic code (Status: Pending; Structural-rules from the boundary_input facet or a runtime placeholder). Zero boundary_input dependencies -> empty table body (valid).

operator-preference per-domain validation sensitivity (GATE-2: Low/Medium/High), scaffold-rendered into quality/validation_gate_config.md. decision_field=true (decision_kind threshold) — a sensitivity-level change alters system pushback behavior. Mandatory group target: always derived; a zero-external-input system records GATE-2='none (no external input domains)' and derives an EMPTY table. Previously unwired.

non-authoritative projection: the health_monitored subset of the canonical dependency record (runtime _derivation_inputs [EXTERNAL_DEPENDENCY_IDENTITY, EXTERNAL_DEPENDENCY_ANNOTATION]), reshaped into the source registry and scaffold-rendered into quality/source_registry.md. The integration-boundary decision lives on the canonical identity record (dropping a monitored source suppresses its monitoring, surfaced there); this is a generated view. ROWS-ONLY (the template owns the 8-col header). DERIVED cells copied from the canonical record: Source name / Type / Purpose / What stops without it; Status defaults 'Pending'. NEVER-synthesized observed-health cells emit a fixed placeholder: Expected behavior + Last verified = '(set at runtime)', Health flag = 'Pending'. Distinct emitted file from the validation gate. Zero health_monitored dependencies -> empty table body (valid).
